Copy:
🕯️𝐒𝐞𝐜𝐫𝐞𝐭𝐬 𝐭𝐨 𝐒𝐮𝐜𝐜𝐞𝐬𝐬𝐟𝐮𝐥 𝐁𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐝𝐢𝐧𝐠 (𝐂𝐚𝐧𝐝𝐥𝐞)🕯️
Learn the process of building your own artisanal candle brand through brand management strategies, customer profiling and hands-on techniques 
✅ Subsidies available (𝐒'𝐩𝐨𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐧 & 𝐏𝐑𝐬 𝐎𝐍𝐋𝐘)
✅ SkillsFuture credits eligible (𝐒'𝐩𝐨𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐧 𝐎𝐍𝐋𝐘)
✅ Up to 70% course fee subsidy (𝐒'𝐩𝐨𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐧 𝐎𝐍𝐋𝐘)
Headline: Build your candle brand
Analysis:
TOFU → MOFU

TOFU (Top of Funnel): Raises awareness among aspiring small business owners or hobbyists.
MOFU (Middle of Funnel): Details on activities (customer profiling, packaging, hands-on creation) help move the viewer closer to decision by showcasing the full learning experience.

Attention: Opens with a compelling question: “Want to learn how to start your own brand?” plus clean visuals with popular design tools (Canva). 

Interest: The ad builds curiosity through its step-by-step flow: concept → form → design → execution. Each scene is a mini-milestone that gives the viewer a peek into the experience.

Desire: Viewers get to visualise themselves building something tangible (candles with their own label). Mention of subsidies and SkillsFuture eligibility boosts practical value and lowers financial friction.

Action: There's no visible “Sign up now” or URL presented in the video itself.

Trust Funnel

Relatability (target audience): Highly relatable for creatives, side hustlers, or career switchers.
Proof: Shows real people taking part
Value: Highlights multiple subsidies and SkillsFuture use.
Accessibility: Approachable tone and visuals, Canva use on the mobile reduces intimidation.

Narrative structure: Step-by-step visual journey from concept to product.
Platform-native: Format and pacing are optimised for Reels, TikTok, IG.
Visual Progression: Creates satisfaction in watching the candle brand come to life.
Ownership & Creation: Emotional payoff from “your brand” with a physical product.
Financial Incentives: Up to 70% off is a strong trigger.
Improvement:
- Lack of CTA in both the video and copy > Add Final CTA Screen (2–3 seconds), add cta in copy and change either headline or description to the cta
- Copy can be stronger by adding more benefits, "🔍 Learn Branding Fundamentals”, “📦 Create Real Products”
- Boost Urgency in Caption or Overlay such as ,"Limited runs monthly”
- The last frame is abrupt. Can do round up like showing everyones's finaly product with the class or an ending screen with cta and more info about class registeration. It feels like they brought me through the entire process and then at the end it's like 'Then what? what do I do now? what the finish product look like?"

Copy:
Learn how to make three cocktails (and drink them) - all under $80! 

• Make three cocktails: Mojito, Cosmopolitan, and Old-Fashioned
• Guided practice making three delicious cocktails (shaken, stirred, and built!)
• A deep dive into the history of cocktails, including the wild world of Prohibition, led by an award-winning bartender.
• A breakdown of all the essential bartending techniques.
• A tasting session to sample your delicious creations.

✨ Browse >200 exciting things to do in Singapore on Seek Sophie!
Headline: Craft and Enjoy 3 Cocktails under $30 with Seek Sophie
Analysis:
1. AIDA Framework
Stage        Analysis
Attention        ✅ Strong opening visuals: The POV shots of shaking cocktails and clinking glasses immediately catch attention. The fast-paced editing, vibrant colours of the drinks, and upbeat music create instant engagement. The line “Learn how to make three cocktails (and drink them) — all under $80!” is a scroll-stopping value hook.
Interest        ✅ The combination of immersive footage and an enthusiastic narrator builds curiosity. The class setup, professional yet casual tone, and visible fun environment maintain viewer interest. The “3 styles of cocktails” framing adds educational intrigue.
Desire        ✅ Emotionally resonant. The warmth of the lighting and group energy makes the viewer want to be there. The testimonial tone (“10/10 experience”, “perfect for dates”) sells both the emotional and experiential benefits.
Action        ✅ The CTA “Book now on Seek Sophie, link in bio” is clear, visible, and matches the platform-native tone. It translates desire into a direct next step.
🟩 Overall AIDA Effectiveness: 9/10
2. Funnel Stage (TOFU–MOFU–BOFU)
Stage: TOFU → MOFU bridge
The ad’s purpose is to attract new users seeking experiences (“Browse >200 things to do in Singapore”).
It also moves them toward consideration, since it demonstrates the actual experience and quality.
The testimonial voice gives a trust-building MOFU quality, preparing viewers for conversion.
3. RACE Framework Position
RACE Stage        Relevance
Reach        ✅ The ad works well to attract new audiences who enjoy social outings or experiential gifts.
Act        ✅ It invites engagement (“Book now”), pushing towards an immediate action.
Convert / Engage        Somewhat indirect — conversion likely happens through the linked Seek Sophie landing page. Follow-up remarketing could drive Engage later.
🟩 Primary Stage: Reach–Act bridge
4. Trust Funnel Assessment
Trust Stage        Assessment
Know        The ad clearly introduces Seek Sophie as a trusted platform for unique experiences.
Like        Strong authenticity — real people, genuine tone, and POV filming build emotional connection.
Trust        Featuring a professional, award-winning bartender adds expert credibility.
Try        The “book now” CTA and clear value (“under $100”) lower barriers to trial.
🟩 Position: High Trust / Try Stage
5. Video Ad Review Checklist (per uploaded guide)
Criterion        Evaluation
1. Hook (0–3 sec)        ✅ Immediate action — shaking cocktail shaker and clinking glasses. Eye-catching and social.
2. Audience clarity        ✅ Clear demographic: young adults seeking fun, affordable activities in Singapore. Tone and language match audience vernacular.
3. Captions / sound design        ✅ Voiceover and upbeat music make it engaging with sound; ensure captions are always visible for muted viewers.
4. Story structure        ✅ Clear arc: Hook → Experience → Host credibility → Value summary → CTA. Pacing is smooth and consistent.
5. Real people        ✅ Real participants + authentic testimonial build trust and relatability.
6. On-screen text        ⚠️ Could further highlight offer (“Under $100”, “Make 3 cocktails”) for emphasis and visual reinforcement.
7. CTA clarity        ✅ “Book now on Seek Sophie, link in bio” — clear and action-oriented.
8. Platform native        ✅ Style fits Instagram Reels / TikTok aesthetics (POV shots, quick cuts, casual narration).
🟩 Checklist Score: 8.5/10 overall effectiveness

6. Behavioural and Emotional Triggers
Trigger        Present?        Notes
FOMO / Urgency        ⚠️ Partial — could use a stronger sense of “limited slots available”.        
Social Proof        ✅ Shown through group enjoyment and “10/10 experience” testimonial.        
Contrast        ✅ Educational and social value for low cost.        
Emotional Cue        ✅ Warm lighting, upbeat tone, laughter — evokes happiness and belonging.        
Colour Psychology        ✅ Warm amber tones (trust, comfort), green from Mojito (freshness), pink from Cosmopolitan (fun, playfulness).        
7. Copy, Headline & Description Review
Headline: “Craft and Enjoy 3 Cocktails under $30 with Seek Sophie”
→ ✅ Clear, benefit-driven, and affordable hook. Suggest capitalising “Under $30” or adding “in Singapore” for geo relevance.
Copy: Engaging and descriptive. Balances value (price) and experience (social, fun).
→ Could shorten or bold key phrases for readability in mobile captions.
Description: Missing — adding a short summary (e.g., “Join a hands-on cocktail workshop led by an award-winning bartender. Perfect for dates and groups.”) would reinforce SEO and engagement.
8. Performance Ratings
Category        Score (/10)        Notes
Engagement Potential        9        Immersive visuals and relatable POV storytelling.
Emotional Impact        9        Warm, fun, authentic.
Conversion Potential        8        Strong CTA but could reinforce urgency or limited availability.
Improvement:
Enhance On-Screen Text:
Add dynamic overlays highlighting “Make 3 cocktails under $100” and “Perfect for dates & friends” to boost recall for muted viewers.

Introduce Soft Urgency:
Lines like “Limited spots each weekend” or “Book before slots run out!” could lift conversions.

Optimise the CTA Visual:
End card with Seek Sophie logo, price highlight, and “Book Now” button animation.

Shorten Mid-Segment Voice Lines:
Slightly tighter pacing could improve retention past 20 seconds.

Add a Quick Reaction Clip:
Show participant delight (“Wow, this tastes amazing!”) to amplify emotional payoff.

Copy:
The most unique @seeksophiesg experience takes you on a journey to appreciate wildlife in a unique and unconventional way. Try taxidermy with a seriously passionate taxidermist. We promise you'll uncover a whole new world for you!

✨  Browse over 200 handpicked experiences that's worth your time in Singapore on Seek Sophie.
Analysis:
1. AIDA Framework
Stage        Analysis
Attention        ✅ The opening instantly arrests attention — close-ups of preserved butterflies, bones, and skeletons. The line “This workshop lets you touch and work with dead animals” is bold, even provocative, making this an exceptionally strong scroll-stopper.
Interest        ✅ The curiosity continues as the video transitions from shock to fascination — viewers discover that it’s a scientific, artistic, and ethical experience. The shots of Vivian’s studio, filled with specimens, visually sustain interest.
Desire        ✅ The narrative builds desire through transformation — from morbid curiosity to appreciation and artistry. The ethical sourcing reassurance is critical; it flips potential scepticism into trust. The finished art pieces appeal emotionally to creative and nature-loving audiences.
Action        ✅ The CTA “Link in bio to book this Seek Sophie experience now” is direct and platform-aligned. It concludes the ad cleanly after satisfying both intrigue and reassurance.

🟩 AIDA Effectiveness: 9.5/10 — one of the strongest openings of any Seek Sophie creative, balanced with solid educational and ethical framing.

2. Funnel Stage (TOFU–MOFU–BOFU)

Stage: TOFU → MOFU

Top of Funnel (TOFU): The provocative hook draws new audiences through curiosity and novelty.

Middle of Funnel (MOFU): The deep dive into Vivian’s background, ethical context, and educational benefits nurture trust and interest.

🟩 Position: Awareness + Consideration hybrid — best used for discovery and retargeting audiences seeking creative or scientific experiences.

3. RACE Framework
RACE Stage        Relevance
Reach        ✅ Extremely strong reach potential through shock/curiosity (“touching dead animals”). The headline drives social shares.
Act        ✅ Encourages interaction through fascination — viewers want to learn more about this unusual class.
Convert        ✅ CTA is clear, but emotional conversion relies on reassurance (ethics + artistry).
Engage        ✅ The “wow” factor and conversation-worthy subject make it highly shareable post-booking.

🟩 Primary Stage: Reach–Act bridge

4. Trust Funnel Positioning
Trust Stage        Assessment
Know        Immediate recognition through striking visuals and narration.
Like        Vivian’s authentic personality and passion for her craft build warmth.
Trust        Strong — credentials shown via The Business Times article and ethical sourcing claims.
Try / Advocate        Viewers who book likely share photos of their finished art pieces, driving advocacy.

🟩 Trust Funnel Level: High Trust + Advocacy Ready

5. Video Ad Review Checklist (from your guide)
Criterion        Evaluation
1. Hook (0–3s)        ✅ Exceptional. The unusual imagery and provocative narration are guaranteed to stop scrolling.
2. Audience clarity        ✅ Clear — targets creatives, curious minds, and experience-seekers who want something different.
3. Sound-off design        ⚠️ Needs captions for narration (many viewers will watch muted). Key lines like “We know it sounds creepy…” should appear as text overlays.
4. Story structure        ✅ Logical flow: Shock → Education → Credibility → Ethics → Beauty → CTA. Excellent narrative pacing.
5. Real people        ✅ Vivian’s presence as an authentic, passionate guide reinforces trust. Participants’ shots add realism.
6. On-screen text        ⚠️ Could better highlight benefits and proof points, e.g. “Ethically sourced specimens” and “Led by zoologist Vivian.”
7. CTA clarity        ✅ Clear and platform-native — “Link in bio to book now.” Consider visually reinforcing this near the end.
8. Platform native        ✅ Works well for Instagram Reels and TikTok; quirky subject matter fits discovery platforms perfectly.

🟩 Overall Execution Score: 9/10

6. Behavioural & Emotional Triggers
Trigger        Present?        Notes
Curiosity / Shock Value        ✅ Central to the ad’s appeal — “work with dead animals” is both shocking and fascinating.        
Education / Empowerment        ✅ Learning and artistry build perceived value.        
Empathy / Ethical Reassurance        ✅ The “ethically sourced” line neutralises discomfort and builds trust.        
Social Proof        ⚠️ Minimal — could feature quick participant testimonial (“I never thought I’d enjoy taxidermy!”).        
FOMO / Novelty        ✅ The “one-of-a-kind” positioning naturally drives exclusivity and curiosity.        
Colour Psychology        ✅ Natural, earthy tones suggest science and authenticity; pops of iridescent butterfly colour evoke wonder and life.        

🟩 Emotional Trigger Strength: 9/10

7. Copy, Headline & Messaging Review

Copy:
“The most unique @seeksophiesg experience takes you on a journey to appreciate wildlife…”
✅ On-brand tone — aspirational, curious, inclusive.
⚠️ Could sharpen the hook for scrollers:

“Learn the art of taxidermy in Singapore — it’s weirdly beautiful, surprisingly ethical, and totally fascinating.”

Headline (implied):
“Taxidermy Workshop with Vivian, the Black Crow Taxidermist” — ✅ clear and intrigue-driven.

Description:
Should reinforce benefits and ethics:

“Discover the beauty in nature’s afterlife. Learn ethical taxidermy and create stunning art under expert guidance.”

8. Performance Ratings
Category        Score (/10)        Notes
Engagement Potential        9.5        Strong curiosity driver; high potential for replay and shares.
Emotional Impact        9        Combines wonder, fascination, and ethical reflection.
Conversion Potential        8.5        Solid, though adding a participant quote or urgency overlay would strengthen it.
Improvement:
Add Captions and Key Phrases On-Screen
Use dynamic overlays for:

“Led by zoologist Vivian 🦋”

“Everything ethically sourced locally”

“Create your own art piece”

Reinforce the CTA Visually
End card with: “Book the Black Crow Taxidermy Workshop on Seek Sophie” — short, animated, and clear.

Incorporate Participant Reaction Clip
Quick soundbite: “This was surprisingly therapeutic!” — boosts relatability and social proof.

Short-Form Variants
Create 15s and 30s edits with the opening “dead animals” hook and ethics line for high TOFU performance.

Leverage Curiosity in Caption Copy
Example:

“Think taxidermy is creepy? Think again. Discover the art, science, and love behind Singapore’s most unique workshop.”
